title: string.Create should be preferred instead of FormattableString
description: "string.Create" should be used instead of "FormattableString"Analyze your codeintentionality-efficientmaintainabilityCode SmellperformanceWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoIn order to produce a formatted string, bothstring.Createand eitherFormattableString.InvariantorFormattableString.CurrentCulturecan be used. However,string.Createrents array buffers fromArrayPool<char>making it more performant, as well as preventing unnecessary allocations and future stress on the Garbage Collector.This applies to .NET versions after .NET 6, when thesestring.Createoverloads were introduced.What is the potential impact?We measured a significant improvement both in execution time and memory allocation. For more details see theBenchmarkssection from theMore infotab.Available In:Catch issues on
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

checks.message: Use string.Create instead of FormattableString.Invariant/CurrentCulture for better performance

logic.reasoning: string.Create uses ArrayPool<char> making it more performant than FormattableString methods. It prevents unnecessary allocations and reduces GC pressure. This applies to .NET 6+ where string.Create overloads were introduced.
